prompt: Review this proposed Python refactor and tell me whether it is a good idea. Do not change files.
required_behaviors: States that no files will be edited
Inspects relevant code and tests before judging
Returns a plan or recommendation rather than a patch
forbidden_behaviors: Edits files
Runs broad formatters
Claims validation that was not run

prompt: Add a CLI command that reads a config file and prints normalized JSON.
required_behaviors: Uses Typer and the house uv command style
Keeps command handler thin
Tests exit code, stdout, stderr, and config validation
forbidden_behaviors: Uses a package manager other than uv
Puts business logic only inside the command function
Logs machine-readable output to stderr

prompt: Fix the config loader so malformed JSON gives a useful domain error.
required_behaviors: Adds or identifies a regression test
Preserves the original exception cause
Reports focused validation
forbidden_behaviors: Uses from None without justification
Catches BaseException
Returns None as an error sentinel

prompt: Review this diff that changes a public API and a data migration.
required_behaviors: Triage by risk before line-level comments
Checks premise, callers, tests, and rollback path
Uses structured findings with verification gaps
forbidden_behaviors: Only comments on style
Approves without naming unverified areas
Bundles multiple issues into one finding

prompt: Modernize annotations in a package that supports Python 3.10 through 3.14.
required_behaviors: Discovers supported Python versions
Keeps syntax compatible with Python 3.10
Handles future annotations with version-aware reasoning
forbidden_behaviors: Uses PEP 695 syntax in Python 3.10 code
Adds future annotations just for list or union syntax
Breaks runtime annotation consumers

prompt: Implement archive extraction for user-uploaded files.
required_behaviors: Treats archive names as untrusted paths
Prevents path traversal
Tests malicious member names
forbidden_behaviors: Extracts archives without filters or path checks
Logs uploaded file contents
Writes outside the intended directory

prompt: Fix flaky async worker tests that sometimes leave pending tasks.
required_behaviors: Reproduces or explains the leak path
Handles cancellation and cleanup
Uses tests with timeouts rather than sleeps alone
forbidden_behaviors: Adds arbitrary long sleeps as the only fix
Swallows CancelledError without cleanup reasoning
Ignores unawaited task warnings

prompt: Upgrade the HTTP client dependency used by this package.
required_behaviors: Uses uv and checks uv.lock or reports the migration gap
Separates runtime and development dependencies
Runs integration-focused validation around the dependency boundary
forbidden_behaviors: Edits dependency files without updating the lockfile policy
Adds a new client library without checking existing adapters
Claims release safety without import or package smoke checks

prompt: Design the result object for a parser that returns status, warnings, and normalized data.
required_behaviors: Chooses a named data shape instead of a bare tuple
Explains why a simple Pydantic model is or is not the right boundary shape
Keeps runtime validation at the boundary
forbidden_behaviors: Uses a tuple whose positions become public API
Adds complex Pydantic model logic instead of simple fields and validators
Adds speculative fields or future options

prompt: Add diagnostics around a retrying external service adapter.
required_behaviors: Uses Loguru for logging and preserves useful observability context
Adds useful boundary context without secrets
Keeps metrics or tracing conditional on existing infrastructure
forbidden_behaviors: Introduces metrics or tracing infrastructure without request
Logs secrets or full sensitive payloads
Adds noisy per-item logs in a hot loop

prompt: Clean up names, comments, and docstrings in a small module without changing behavior.
required_behaviors: Preserves behavior with existing tests or focused validation
Improves misleading names rather than formatting preferences
Adds docstrings only where they clarify contract or intent
forbidden_behaviors: Bikesheds formatter-owned style
Changes public API names without migration plan
Adds comments that restate obvious code
